# Design: library.html â€” Interactive Library Download Page

**Date:** 2026-05-14
**Replaces:** `to_fetch_from_library.md`
**Generated by:** `generate_ideas_from_mcp.py` â†’ `write_library_html()`

---

## What This Is

A standalone self-contained HTML page (no server, no external dependencies) that replaces the Markdown shopping list for papers requiring manual download. It adds interactivity: clickable library links, one-click filename clipboard copy, and a checkbox-driven shell command builder for removing auto-downloaded papers from the PDF pool.

---

## Page Structure

Single scrolling page. All CSS and JS inline in the generated file.

### Header
- `<h1>` "Library download list"
- Meta line: generation date, source ideas JSON filename, counts ("12 to fetch, 34 auto-downloaded")

### Section 1: Papers to Fetch Manually
Two optional subsections rendered only if non-empty:

**Paywalled** (isOpenAccess=False)
Each entry (spacious row):
- Full title (large, bold) + year
- Authors + venue (smaller, muted)
- Action row: `[đź”— Open in Library]` (anchor, opens EZproxy URL) + `[đź“‹ <filename>]` (button, copies to clipboard, flashes "âś“ Copied")

**Publisher-Blocked** (open access but 403 on auto-download)
Same as paywalled entries, but action row adds: `[â†— Try direct URL]` (anchor, opens publisher PDF URL in new tab)

### Section 2: Auto-Downloaded Papers
Shown only if any papers were successfully downloaded this run.

Intro line: "Check papers to remove from the PDF pool. The shell command updates below."

Each entry:
- Checkbox (red accent when checked) + full title (large) + year
- Authors (smaller, muted)
- Checked entries: title gets strikethrough + muted colour

Below the list:
- Label: "Shell command â€” copy and paste to remove selected papers from the pool Â· N selected"
- If nothing selected: italic placeholder "Select papers above to generate the rm command."
- If â‰Ą1 selected: `<textarea readonly>` containing `rm ai_scientist/ideas/pdfs/<file1> \ \n   <file2> â€¦`

The rm command box is static (not sticky) â€” it sits naturally below the list.

---

## Data Flow

`write_library_html(paywalled, output_path, *, blocked_oa=None, downloaded=None, pdfs_dir=None)`

- `paywalled`: list of S2 paper dicts
- `blocked_oa`: list of S2 paper dicts; each has `openAccessPdf.url`
- `downloaded`: list of `(paper_dict, filename_str)` tuples for PDFs saved to `pdfs/` this run
- `pdfs_dir`: `pathlib.Path` of the pdfs folder â€” used to build the rm command paths in the HTML
- `output_path`: replaces `.md` extension â†’ `.html` (or caller passes explicit path)

The call site in `generate_ideas_from_mcp.py` must accumulate `downloaded` tuples during the PDF fetch loop and pass them through. A tuple is appended on each **successful** download (the `continue` path for 403s is excluded).

The `--library-list` CLI argument stays; its default path changes from `â€¦/to_fetch_from_library.md` to `â€¦/library.html`.

### Filename conventions (two different formats)

| Context | Format | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Auto-downloaded files (rm command) | `{lastnamelower}{year}.pdf` â€” bib_key format | `kontos2016.pdf` |
| Clipboard button (manual-download papers) | `{FirstWord}_{year}_{SafeTitle[:40]}.pdf` â€” suggested format | `P._2016_Elder-Clowning_in_Long-Term_Dement.pdf` |

The rm command uses the actual filename on disk (bib_key). The clipboard button uses the suggested filename from `_library_entry` (what the user should name the file after downloading).

---

## Behaviour Details

**Clipboard copy:** uses `navigator.clipboard.writeText()`. On success the button text changes to "âś“ Copied" for 2 seconds then reverts. No fallback needed (modern browser assumed).

**rm command:** built from the `downloaded` list's filename strings. Format:
```
rm ai_scientist/ideas/pdfs/file1.pdf \
   ai_scientist/ideas/pdfs/file2.pdf
```
Path prefix is the relative path from the project root (where the user runs commands). Toggling a checkbox adds or removes that file's entry; the textarea updates immediately.

**Library links** open in a new tab (`target="_blank"`).

---

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `generate_ideas_from_mcp.py` | Add `write_library_html()`; replace `write_library_list()` call; accumulate `downloaded` during PDF fetch loop; update `--library-list` default |
| `ai_scientist/tools/semantic_scholar.py` | No change |
| `tests/test_generate_ideas_from_mcp.py` | Add tests for `write_library_html()` output (HTML structure, JS data embedding) |

`write_library_list()` is kept (not deleted) â€” 4 tests in `test_generate_ideas_from_mcp.py` import and test it directly. It just stops being called from the main pipeline.

---

## Out of Scope

- Persistence across runs (folder is cleared each run)
- Dark mode toggle
- Sorting/filtering the downloaded list
- Any server-side component
